justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime migration via Strangler Fig with synchronous dual-write, (2) Phases clearly defined — schema setup, backfill, dual-write, shadow-read/cutover, decommission, (3) Rollback plan at each phase via feature flags and MongoDB kept warm as hot standby, (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified as high severity with mitigation (audit all 8M documents for schema variance before designing PostgreSQL schema), (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency (~5-10ms per write) and operational overhead of maintaining two stores.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed phased migration with four distinct phases, each with clear entry/exit criteria and rollback capability
Shadow-read pattern for validation before cutover is a sophisticated addition beyond basic dual-write
Repair queue for failed secondary writes addresses partial failure scenarios during dual-write
Reconciliation job with 48-hour zero-divergence gate before cutover is a strong data consistency safeguard
MongoDB document schema variance identified as high severity risk with concrete mitigation (variety.js or custom aggregation audit)
Backfill worker designed as idempotent with cursor-based pagination and UPSERT semantics — handles writes during backfill correctly
Correctly sized the problem — noted 200 writes/min makes synchronous dual-write viable over CDC, avoiding unnecessary infrastructure
Repository Abstraction Layer provides lasting architectural value beyond the migration itself
Output is extremely verbose — could be more concise while retaining the same architectural quality
